summary: AI machine vision and process control for food production lines.
editor_note: Food factories do not need prettier dashboards; they need the line to catch drift before a batch becomes waste. Polysense pairs inline machine vision with Qualify inspection, Intelligence analytics, and AutoControl adjustments. Unlike sample-based QC, the bet is every product checked in realtime. The risk is whether $10.7M Seed expansion keeps pace with plant-by-plant variation.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes AI-native machine vision and process control for food production lines that catch quality drift before waste occurs.
The product navigation exposes Qualify, Intelligence, and AutoControl modules for inspection, analytics, and process adjustment.
The funding report says Polysense secured $10.7M to expand its food-manufacturing quality-control platform.

market_context.primary_user: Food manufacturers, plant managers, production engineers, and quality-control teams.
market_context.differentiation: Polysense applies inline AI vision and process feedback to food-production drift rather than relying only on sample-based quality checks.
market_context.why_now: Food processors are under pressure to reduce waste, protect margins, and prove quality without adding more manual inspection labor.

risks.claim: The falsifiable risk is whether the system adapts across heterogeneous food lines quickly enough for repeatable deployment economics.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official product pages and seed funding report.
